name: Vast.ai
description: Vast.ai GPU cloud marketplace API for searching live GPU offers, creating and managing instances, SSH access, templates, volumes, billing, GPU market metrics, serverless endpoints, deployments, and notification webhooks.

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Vast.ai API key from cloud.vast.ai/manage-keys. A scoped key needs user_read and instance_read; creation and lifecycle tools also need instance_write.

tools.name: get_current_user
tools.description: Get the authenticated Vast.ai account, including user ID, email, current credit balance, and account SSH key information. Use this to verify credentials before renting resources.

tools.name: search_offers
tools.description: Search the live Vast.ai GPU marketplace. Filter values use operator objects such as {"gte":1}, {"eq":true}, or {"in":["RTX 4090"]}. Offers are ephemeral: use the returned offer id immediately with create_instance and retry another result if it becomes unavailable.

type.description: ondemand is fixed price; bid is interruptible; reserved supports reservation pricing.

gpu_ram.description: Total GPU VRAM in MB in the REST API, e.g. {"gte":24000}.

dph_total.description: Total hourly price filter in USD, e.g. {"lte":0.5}.

geolocation.description: Location filter using Vast's geolocation strings.

compute_cap.description: CUDA compute capability multiplied by 10, e.g. {"gte":80}.

allocated_storage.description: Disk allocation in GB used when calculating offer pricing.

tools.name: list_instances
tools.description: List owned instances with status, GPU, pricing, SSH/Jupyter connection fields, and pagination. select_filters, select_cols, and order_by are JSON strings because Vast expects JSON in query parameters.

select_cols.description: JSON array of returned columns, or ["*"].

tools.name: get_instance
tools.description: Get one instance. For connection details inspect actual_status, ssh_host, ssh_port, public_ipaddr, ports, jupyter_token, and image_runtype. Poll until actual_status is running; exited, unknown, or offline will not become ready without intervention.

tools.name: create_instance
tools.description: Rent an offer and create an instance. First call search_offers, then pass its id as offer_id. The response's new_contract is the new instance ID. Use image for direct configuration or template_hash_id for a template. For environment variables and ports, env is an object such as {"HF_TOKEN":"...","-p 8000:8000":"1"}.

image.description: Docker image, e.g. nvidia/cuda:12.4.1-runtime-ubuntu22.04. Optional only when template_hash_id supplies it.

price.description: Hourly bid for interruptible offers only. Omit for on-demand.

env.description: Environment variables and Docker port mappings.

cancel_unavail.description: Fail fast if the offer cannot start immediately.

volume_info.description: Existing volume: {create_new:false,volume_id,mount_path}. New volume from offer: {create_new:true,volume_id,size,mount_path}.

tools.name: manage_instance
tools.description: Start or stop an instance and/or update its label. Stopping pauses compute charges but storage charges continue.

tools.name: destroy_instance
tools.description: Permanently destroy an instance and its local data. This is irreversible; use manage_instance with state=stopped when temporary shutdown is intended.

tools.name: reboot_instance
tools.description: Restart an instance container without relinquishing GPU priority.

tools.name: recycle_instance
tools.description: Destroy and recreate the container in place from the image without relinquishing GPU priority. Container-local changes are lost.

tools.name: change_instance_bid
tools.description: Change the hourly bid of an interruptible instance.

client_id.description: Optional Vast client/user ID when required by the account context.

tools.name: prepay_instance
tools.description: Deposit prepaid credit into a reserved instance to obtain a duration-based discount.

tools.name: execute_instance_command
tools.description: Execute a constrained command on an instance. The API returns a result URL/status rather than maintaining an interactive shell.

tools.name: request_instance_logs
tools.description: Request recent instance logs. tail controls the number of lines; filter narrows log text; daemon_logs selects daemon rather than container logs.

tools.name: list_account_ssh_keys
tools.description: List SSH public keys registered on the Vast.ai account. Register keys before creating VM instances; Docker instances can also attach keys afterward.

tools.name: create_account_ssh_key
tools.description: Register an SSH public key on the account. Vast adds account keys to current and future compatible instances.

tools.name: update_account_ssh_key
tools.description: Replace a registered account SSH public key.

tools.name: delete_account_ssh_key
tools.description: Delete a registered account SSH key.

tools.name: list_instance_ssh_keys
tools.description: List SSH keys currently attached to one instance.

tools.name: attach_instance_ssh_key
tools.description: Attach an OpenSSH public key directly to a running Docker instance.

tools.name: detach_instance_ssh_key
tools.description: Detach one SSH key from an instance.

tools.name: search_templates
tools.description: Search Vast.ai launch templates. select_filters, select_cols, and order_by must be JSON strings because the API accepts them as encoded query values.

select_filters.description: JSON filter object with eq/neq/lt/lte/gt/gte/in/notin operators.

tools.name: create_template
tools.description: Create a reusable Vast.ai instance template. Template env uses Docker flag syntax, unlike create_instance env which uses an object.

tools.name: edit_template
tools.description: Edit mutable fields of an existing template identified by hash_id.

tools.name: delete_template
tools.description: Delete a template by numeric template ID.

tools.name: list_volumes
tools.description: List persistent volumes rented by the current user.

tools.name: search_volumes
tools.description: Search available persistent volume offers. q is Vast's JSON-encoded query/filter string; limit is sent in the request body.

tools.name: rent_volume
tools.description: Rent/create a persistent volume from a volume offer ID.

tools.name: delete_volume
tools.description: Permanently delete a rented persistent volume.

tools.name: list_charges
tools.description: List per-instance GPU, storage, and bandwidth charges. Query fields that contain JSON must be passed as JSON strings.

tools.name: list_invoices
tools.description: List account billing transactions such as card top-ups, transfers, payouts, and crypto payments.

tools.name: get_gpu_market_metrics
tools.description: Get a current marketplace snapshot of GPU supply, demand, and pricing.

tools.name: get_gpu_locations
tools.description: Get the geographic distribution of GPUs available on the Vast.ai marketplace.

tools.name: get_gpu_market_history
tools.description: Get time-series GPU supply, demand, and pricing metrics.

tools.name: list_serverless_endpoints
tools.description: List Vast serverless endpoint jobs.

tools.name: create_serverless_endpoint
tools.description: Create a serverless endpoint. Pair it with one or more workergroups that define templates and GPU search criteria.

tools.name: update_serverless_endpoint
tools.description: Update scaling settings or name for a serverless endpoint.

tools.name: delete_serverless_endpoint
tools.description: Delete a serverless endpoint and its associated workergroups.

tools.name: list_workergroups
tools.description: List serverless workergroups and their autoscaling/template configuration.

tools.name: create_workergroup
tools.description: Create a serverless workergroup attached to an endpoint. search_params selects marketplace workers; launch_args configures launches.

tools.name: update_workergroup
tools.description: Update a serverless workergroup's template, GPU search, launch, or scaling settings.

tools.name: delete_workergroup
tools.description: Delete a serverless workergroup.

tools.name: list_deployments
tools.description: List owned Vast serverless deployments and their endpoint state.

tools.name: get_deployment
tools.description: Get one serverless deployment, endpoint state, and worker counts.

tools.name: start_deployment
tools.description: Start or restart a serverless deployment and its autoscaler.

tools.name: stop_deployment
tools.description: Stop a serverless deployment without deleting it.

tools.name: list_notification_types
tools.description: List notification event type keys available to the account. Use returned keys as event_types when creating or updating a webhook.

tools.name: list_notification_webhooks
tools.description: List notification webhooks registered on the Vast.ai account.

tools.name: create_notification_webhook
tools.description: Create a notification webhook for one or more event type keys. Store webhook.webhook_secret from the response immediately; Vast uses it to sign X-Vast-Signature-256.

tools.name: update_notification_webhook
tools.description: Update a notification webhook name, destination URL, or subscribed event types.

tools.name: delete_notification_webhook
tools.description: Delete a Vast.ai notification webhook.

tools.name: rotate_notification_webhook_secret
tools.description: Rotate a webhook signing secret. Store the newly returned secret immediately.

tools.name: test_notification_webhook
tools.description: Send a test delivery to a registered webhook using Vast's normal payload and signature headers.
